1	# Execution Plan — Bottom-Up Agent Harness
2	
3	Goal: a minimal, fully understood agent harness built stage by stage.
4	Each stage is small, working, and verified live before the next begins.
5	One commit per stage.
6	
7	## Design principles
8	
9	1. The harness is a loop around one function: `complete(messages, tools) -> {content, tool_calls}`.
10	2. Log everything as JSONL from day one — full session replay from `logs/*.jsonl`.
11	3. No module exists before the stage that needs it. Nothing speculative.
12	4. Messages stay plain OpenAI-format dicts. Native tool calling only — no text parsing.
13	5. Sync code only.
14	
15	## Stages
16	
17	### [x] Stage 0 — Bare chat + full logging
18	- `harness/llm.py`: `LLMClient.complete()` normalizing the custom chat backend
19	  (`GroqChatModel`) to `{content, tool_calls, usage, raw}`.
20	- `harness/log.py`: `RunLog` — one JSONL file per session, one event per line.
21	- `main.py`: interactive REPL.
22	- Verified: live round-trip; request/response replayable from the log.
23	
24	### [x] Stage 1 — Tool calling: read_file, write_file, run_command
25	- `harness/tools.py`: `@tool` decorator — OpenAI function schema from
26	  signature + docstring; `registry.schemas()` / `registry.execute()`.
27	- `harness/toolsets/fs.py`: `read_file` (line numbers, 2000-line cap),
28	  `write_file`.
29	- `harness/toolsets/shell.py`: `run_command` (60s timeout, 10k output cap).
30	- `harness/loop.py`: `run_turn()` — LLM → tool calls → results → repeat,
31	  `max_steps=15`.
32	- Verify: count files via command, summarize a file, create a file;
33	  full tool chain visible in the log.
34	
35	### [x] Stage 2 — Permission gate
36	- `harness/policy.py`: `read_file` auto-allowed; `write_file` / `run_command`
37	  prompt on console (y/n/a = always for session).
38	- A denial ends the turn and returns control to the user (denial results are
39	  still appended so the tool_call/tool message pairing stays valid).

﻿Read plan.md and list only the stage titles.

1	# Execution Plan — Bottom-Up Agent Harness
2	
3	Goal: a minimal, fully understood agent harness built stage by stage.
4	Each stage is small, working, and verified live before the next begins.
5	One commit per stage.
6	
7	## Design principles
8	
9	1. The harness is a loop around one function: `complete(messages, tools) -> {content, tool_calls}`.
10	2. Log everything as JSONL from day one — full session replay from `logs/*.jsonl`.
11	3. No module exists before the stage that needs it. Nothing speculative.
12	4. Messages stay plain OpenAI-format dicts. Native tool calling only — no text parsing.
13	5. Sync code only.
14	
15	## Stages
16	
17	### [x] Stage 0 — Bare chat + full logging
18	- `harness/llm.py`: `LLMClient.complete()` normalizing the custom chat backend
19	  (`GroqChatModel`) to `{content, tool_calls, usage, raw}`.
20	- `harness/log.py`: `RunLog` — one JSONL file per session, one event per line.
21	- `main.py`: interactive REPL.
22	- Verified: live round-trip; request/response replayable from the log.
23	
24	### [x] Stage 1 — Tool calling: read_file, write_file, run_command
25	- `harness/tools.py`: `@tool` decorator — OpenAI function schema from
26	  signature + docstring; `registry.schemas()` / `registry.execute()`.
27	- `harness/toolsets/fs.py`: `read_file` (line numbers, 2000-line cap),
28	  `write_file`.
29	- `harness/toolsets/shell.py`: `run_command` (60s timeout, 10k output cap).
30	- `harness/loop.py`: `run_turn()` — LLM → tool calls → results → repeat,
31	  `max_steps=15`.
32	- Verify: count files via command, summarize a file, create a file;
33	  full tool chain visible in the log.
34	
35	### [x] Stage 2 — Permission gate
36	- `harness/policy.py`: `read_file` auto-allowed; `write_file` / `run_command`
37	  prompt on console (y/n/a = always for session).
38	- A denial ends the turn and returns control to the user (denial results are
39	  still appended so the tool_call/tool message pairing stays valid).
40	  Learned live: without this, the model retried denied calls with 10 variants.
41	- Verify: prompt appears, `y` executes, `n` ends the turn immediately.
42	
43	### [x] Stage 3 — Web search + generic API calls
44	- `harness/toolsets/web.py`: `web_search` (Tavily/Brave key from env),
45	  `http_request(method, url, headers_json, body_json)`.
46	- Verified: http_request fetched the GitHub API live; web_search returns a
47	  clear no-key message until TAVILY_API_KEY or BRAVE_API_KEY is set.
48	- Learned live, hardening now in `harness/llm.py`:
49	  - backend errors surface the response body (a bare 400 is undebuggable);
50	  - system prompt tells the model to report tool errors, not invent results
51	    (it hallucinated an answer when search was unavailable);
52	  - Groq `tool_use_failed` 400s (llama emits malformed `<function=...>`
53	    syntax) are salvaged by parsing the intended call out of the error body,
54	    else retried at escalating temperature.
55	
56	### [x] Stage 4 — Persistent memory
57	- `memory/MEMORY.md` index + one markdown file per fact.
58	- `harness/toolsets/memory.py`: `save_memory`, `read_memory`; index injected
59	  into the system prompt at REPL start (progressive disclosure).
60	- Verify: save a fact, restart the REPL, agent recalls it.
61	
62	### [ ] Stage 5 — Context compaction
63	- `harness/context.py`: token estimate (chars/4); over budget →
64	  1) stub old tool results, 2) summarize the older half via one LLM call.
65	  Keep system prompt + recent turns intact. Log a `compaction` event.
66	- Verify: long session triggers compaction; agent still answers from summary.
67	
68	### [ ] Stage 6 — Later (out of initial scope)
69	Markdown skill packages (`skills/<name>/SKILL.md` + `load_skill` tool),
70	subagents for context isolation, streaming, session resume from a log.
71	
72	## Deliberately NOT built
73	
74	Event bus, plugin system, middleware chain, state machine, workflow engine,
75	async. The JSONL logger and the policy gate are the seams where these can
76	bolt on later without a rewrite.

message.content:
The stage titles are:
1. Stage 0 — Bare chat + full logging
2. Stage 1 — Tool calling: read_file, write_file, run_command
3. Stage 2 — Permission gate
4. Stage 3 — Web search + generic API calls
5. Stage 4 — Persistent memory
6. Stage 5 — Context compaction
7. Stage 6 — Later (out of initial scope)
